name: Fluid Sim 2D
description:
Density-displacement particle fluid simulation, organized into named node groups so the whole instrument reads at a glance. The flat graph (atom-decomposed from the legacy fluid_seed / fluid_simulate / fluid_gradient_rotate bundles) is unchanged in behavior — the groups are a presentation layer that flattens away at load, byte-identical to the ungrouped form. Card bindings reach inner nodes by stable nodeId, so grouping never touches the performance surface.

The top level is the feedback loop and the data flowing through it:
- Particle State (node.array_feedback) is the one piece of frame-to-frame state. It holds last frame's particles, hands them to Move Particles and Render Density, takes the moved particles back, and reseeds from Spawn Particles when Clip Triggers fires a reset.
- Spawn Particles cycles through 7 hard-edged patterns (cluster, lines, rings, cross, spiral, edge ring) on each clip trigger and rejection-samples the chosen pattern into a particle buffer. The pattern math is editable WGSL — add an 8th case to grow the set.
- Move Particles is the per-particle integrator: sample the flow field, add density-adaptive simplex turbulence, anti-clump where particles crowd, take a frame-rate-normalised Euler step, wrap at the edges, then apply any injection burst.
- Render Density splats the particles into a density image for display and smooths a downsampled copy that feeds next frame's forces.
- Flow Field turns that smoothed density into the force that pushes particles: central-difference gradient, scaled by Flow, rotated by Curl, then blurred.
- Display zooms and tone-maps the density to the screen.
- Resolution Scaling derives the resolution-independent numbers the pipeline needs from the canvas size and the sliders: particle count, scatter energy, blur radii, display zoom and intensity.
- Clip Triggers is the performance engine. A clip trigger fires a decay envelope; the Trigger Mode dial routes that envelope to exactly one of five nested behaviors — Turbulence Burst, Rotation Flip, Flow Reversal, Pattern Reset, Inject Burst — which is why those live as sub-groups sharing one envelope and one mode selector.

Parity deltas vs the legacy bundles are unchanged from the decomposition: seed patterns are density textures rejection-sampled into particles (statistically distributed, not exact placement); fill scales the live particle count; the pattern index advances on every trigger regardless of mode; injection is applied per-particle post-integration.
